methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Cuby Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Cover, a Series B-stage housing and construction platform founded by Alexis Rivas, has raised over $100M to date. The relevant comp universe in Housing & Construction is sparse: zero public comparables and two private peers (ICON and Cuby Technologies), insufficient for robust peer-median triangulation.

Only one valuation method proved applicable: stage-progression analysis. Using the $100M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $190M. The peer-median approach was rejected due to insufficient same-stage and comparable-tier datapoints within the sector; relying on all-sector medians would introduce material cross-sector bias.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single method, supports a valuation range of $162M (low) to $218M (high), with a median point of $190M. Key uncertainties include the reliability of total raised as a valuation proxy absent disclosed post-money terms, the applicability of standard step-up multiples to an early-stage, capital-intensive construction tech company, and the absence of direct peer benchmarking. Resolution of these uncertainties—particularly access to recent Housing & Construction Series B+ comp data—would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
